name: Mexican Kings Cake--Rosca De Reyes
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp Orange flower water; OR orange juice
Dried beans; or 2, covered in foil
recipeInstructions:
Combine yeast and water in a small bowl, stirring until dissolved. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the milk, sugar, and butter, stirring until dissolved. Add salt, grated lemon rind, eggs, orange flower water (or juice), and yeast mixture. Blend thoroughly. Beat in 3 cups of flour to make a smooth batter. Add raisins, pecans, and sufficient flour to make a soft dough. Turn out onto a floured surface and knead 8 minutes or until smooth. Form into a ball and place in a warm buttered bowl, turning to coat the top. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and towel. Let rise until doubled.
The coffee cake may be formed in two different shapes: free form round loaves or molded rings. Punch down the dough and divide in half. Knead bean(s) into one or both halves. Form into loaves and place on a buttered baking sheet. Cover and let rise until doubled about 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake loaves for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°F (175°C) and bake for another 30 minutes. Let loaves cool on wire racks.
To prepare rings, divide dough in half and roll each piece into a rope 24-26 inches long. Place in buttered ring or savarin molds, pinching ends together. Cover and let rise until doubled about 1 hour.
Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake rings for 40 minutes. Remove and let cool on wire racks.
Frosting:
Combine 1 cup confectioners' sugar with 2 teaspoons lemon juice and enough water to make a paste.
